Design and implement enterprise integration solutions connecting diverse internal and external systems across Family Dollar's retail operations using iPaaS platforms, APIs, and cloud services.
...
## Job description
...
The **Integration Engineer** will design, build, and maintain integration solutions that connect diverse internal and external systems across the Family Dollar organization. This role ensures seamless data flow, reliable system communication, and scalable automation using APIs, middleware, and cloud‑based integration platforms. The Integration Engineer will work closely with cross‑functional teams to translate business requirements into robust technical solutions while upholding high standards for performance, security, and data integrity. The ideal candidate combines strong technical integration expertise with an understanding of retail business processes and data flows.
...
* **Integration Solutions:** Design and implement scalable integration patterns, including event‑driven architecture, API‑led connectivity, and cloud‑native workflows.
...
* **API Development & Management:** Build, document, and maintain REST APIs; manage API lifecycle and enforce security best practices (OAuth2, OIDC).
...
* **iPaaS Configuration:** Configure, orchestrate, and optimize integrations using platforms such as Sterling B2Bi, Informatica and Oracle Integration Cloud (OIC).
...
* **Data Mapping & Transformation:** Develop logic to translate and transform data across formats (JSON, XML, CSV, EDI) and systems.
...
& Testing:**

...
* **Cross‑Functional Collaboration:** Partner with Supply Chain, Merchandising, Store Operations, e‑commerce, HR, Finance, Digital & Analytics, and IT teams to translate business requirements into scalable technical solutions.
...
* **Education:** Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Software Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.
...
* **Experience:** 3–5+ years in systems integration, software development, or cloud‑native application engineering, ideally supporting retail or consumer‑goods environments.
...
* 3+ years’ experience in writing integrations from various source/destinations like databases, files, message queues/topics, APIs required.
...
* **API & Middleware Expertise:** Strong knowledge of REST, SOAP, JSON, XML, and one of the middleware/iPaaS platforms such as Sterling B2Bi, Informatica and Oracle Integration Cloud (OIC).
...
* **Programming Skills:** Proficiency in Python or Java, PL/SQL.
...
* **Cloud Knowledge:** Hands‑on experience with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), or Google Cloud Platform (GCP), or Azure.
...
* **Data & ETL:** Strong SQL skills and experience with ETL/ELT pipelines or platforms such as Informatica or Talend.
...
* **DevOps Tools:** Familiarity with Git, CI/CD pipelines, Docker, Kubernetes, and API testing tools (Postman, SoapUI, JMeter).
...
**Preferred Technical Background**
Preferred but not required 
* Experience integrating enterprise systems such as **SAP**, **Oracle RMS**, **Workday, **or **Lawson Finance**.

* Exposure to modern cloud integration services.

* Experience in integrations pertaining to Composable Architectures, AI Agent orchestrations, legacy modernization

results.title: Dollar Tree AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Dollar Tree has positioned itself as an AI-enabled retailer, leveraging artificial intelligence across workforce management, energy optimization, and loss prevention initiatives [1]. The company has formed strategic partnerships with Legion Technologies for AI-powered workforce management, BrainBox AI for HVAC optimization, and Agilence for data analytics and fraud detection [2]. In its 2025 Investor Day, CEO Mike Creedon emphasized a 'cultural shift' from decades-old legacy systems to AI-enabled platforms for assortment planning, inventory visibility, and workforce management [3]. The company has achieved significant operational improvements through AI, including $1.03 million in energy cost savings through BrainBox AI's HVAC optimization across 600 stores and is exploring agentic AI for hiring process automation [4].
...

In these last several months I've been on a similar AI journey as most of you. I was blown away by how quickly I could ship a fairly complex software solution. In my case, a mobile app on Android and iPhone with a Google cloud backend using Gemini image creation. About 4 days, start to finish, and I was distributing it to peers and colleagues.
...
I pivoted to autonomous agents on my LAN to get my hands dirty. Working in Claude to create these agents — that were not Claude agents. They could run without AI but knew when to engage it. Sometimes Claude, sometimes Gemini, depending on the task. At first I gave them traditional roles. SRE. Development. SecOps. They could read and send emails, create and assign tickets, chat on Teams. (Actually Mattermost.) They all had job descriptions, names, memories, and back-stories. TTS piped them to Mattermost so I could hear them talk. It was nuts.
...
But they didn't do a lot. My LAN doesn't need much attention. I built an agent for something I did have — my recording studio. I created Diane, my studio assistant. I taught her all my gear. She has manuals, user tips, remembers what I used last session. Recently I fed her all the routing — snakes, patch-bays, everything. She uploads songs from my library to Gemini for production info and lyrics. I invested time creating her. Now she saves me a lot of it.
...
Diane was the first. I now have Etta, my astrophotography assistant. She sends commands to my telescopes. Runs batch capture. Knows the best targets each night. A shop agent that tells me which router bits to use for a project. An electronics agent that turns an old '59 Bassman schematic into something modern, then matches it to what I have in inventory. An editor that fixes my punctuation, then gives me a hard time when I tell instead of show. A media library agent. He schedules and reviews like Siskel.
...
my Chief of Staff (Stroud) to onboard
...
Next thing I
...
s introducing them on
...
team channel.
...
I tell people learning AI is bit-flipping. Each insight flips a bit. You progress one flip at a time. A key bit that flipped for me was realizing what I wanted was to replace anything mundane I was spending time on. There are a lot of steps to things I do that keep me from the payoff.
...
AI isn't just a tool that writes a good email or generates a crazy amount of code. It's those things, but for me it's a way to use my tokens — Jay tokens — on the most important things in my life, and still get a lot done. Watch more classic movies with my wife. Spend less time searching for amp schematics. Know what's in my inventory and not buy another half-inch round-over bit. The list goes on.
...
If you're not leveraging AI at home now, you will be. This won't be for everyone, but those I see starting to do it are accelerating their skills at work. Problem-solving is the skill. It compounds.

results.title: CIR/FI Winston
highlights:
Talent Sourcing Leader for Field and Corp. Openings @Dollar Tree: AI-Enhanced Recruiting |Prompt Engineering| |Exec. Search |Change Champion| ERG Mentorship| Leveraging AI & Data-Driven Tools for Smarter Hiring
...
I specialize in talent sourcing with a strong focus on integrating AI-driven tools (ChatGPT, Indeed, LinkedIn Recruiter AI, HireEZ,, etc.) to optimize candidate outreach, streamline sourcing, and improve quality of hire. By combining traditional recruiting strategies with AI technology, I build stronger pipelines and deliver faster, more efficient hiring outcomes. With over 7 years of talent acquisition sourcing experience, I am currently a Leadership Sourcing Expert at Dollar Tree, the leading operator of discount variety stores in North America. I am responsible for discovering, recruiting, assessing, and providing a short list of qualified candidates for all external hiring opportunities, ranging from field and corporate leadership level roles. As a Certified Internet Recruiter (CIR), I leverage my skills in sourcing, screening, building relationships, and retaining candidates, as well as my analytical and problem-solving abilities, to deliver high-quality results and meet the organizational goals and priorities. I am passionate about engaging with passive and active talent, understanding their needs and motivations, and providing them with the best possible career opportunities. I also stay up to date on the latest industry trends and best practices and proactively immerse myself in the retail sector to gain insights and knowledge. My mission is to help Dollar Tree grow and thrive by attracting and retaining the best talent in the market.
...
Help with training, leading meetings and reporting for Sourcers Exp. in a Corp TA, exec search, or sourcing-focused role. Prof in sourcing tools and techniques, including Boolean logic, X-ray searches, LI Recruiter, Chrome ext, and niche talent platforms. Exp working with ATS (Workday). Intermediate skills in Excel and data mngmt, including data mapping and analysis. Familiarity with SEO and data scraping tools and techniques. Ability to manage competing priorities and adapt in a fast-paced, dynamic env. Strong comm and interpersonal skills with a client-focused approach. Responsible for discovery of active and passive talent. Source, recruit, assess and provide a short list of qualified candidates for all ext hiring opportunities. Resp: Partner with Sr. Recruiters & hiring leaders to understand talent needs and priorities (RD, DM, SM, ) to ensure successful recruitment efforts. Responsible for front-end recruitment- recruit, interview, recommend, and hire best in class talent. Build a candidate network that relies on pro-active sourcing techniques and on-going candidate engagement. Recommend recruitment ideas and strategies that build a talent readiness for the organization. Proactively immerse myself in the business and culture to enhance all recruitment efforts and identify potential challenges. Developed reputation as trusted advisor to my field partners based on market knowledge of geographic areas. Description: Demonstrates a good understanding of business objectives and is able to match the right candidate with the right role based on business objectives. Has a key understanding of the available talent within the retail landscape and is able to use data to drive talent sourcing strategies Behaviors:

AI Data Scientist | Jobflarely

# AI Data Scientist
...
Burlington Stores, Inc. is a growing company committed to being an employer of choice. They are seeking a Junior AI Data Scientist to support various business areas by analyzing data and developing AI-driven solutions to enhance retail operations and customer experiences.
...
- Work with stakeholders throughout the organization including Merchandising, Allocations, Marketing, IT and Supply Chain Analytics to assist in developing and implementing AI-powered solutions
- Mine and analyze structured and unstructured data from the data warehouse
- Support prompt engineering and optimization for large language models (LLMs)
- Integrate AI services via APIs and cloud AI platforms
- Keep up to date with latest technology trends
- Work closely with senior data scientists to expand technical and functional knowledge as well as apply best practices
- Coordinate with different functional teams to identify AI automation opportunities
- Develop processes and tools to monitor and analyze model performance and data accuracy
- Translate complex analytical findings into clear business recommendations
...
- Bachelor's degree in AI, Data Science, Computer Science, or related field
- 0–2 years of experience in AI, data analytics, or related technical role
- Knowledge in Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and AI model evaluation concepts
- Exposure to cloud AI platforms (Azure AI, AWS AI services, or GCP Vertex AI)
- 2+ years Data Science experience with extensive background in building ML models, data mining and statistical analysis
- Strong interpersonal and relationship building skills
- Strong written and verbal communication skills

About Team The Catalog Data Science Team at Walmart Global Tech is focused on using the latest research in generative AI (GenAI), artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), statistics, deep learning, computer vision and optimization to implement solutions that ensure Walmart’s product catalog is accurate, complete, and optimized for customer experience. Our team tackles complex data science and ML engineering challenges related to product classification, attribute extraction, trust & safety, and catalog optimization, empowering next-generation retail use cases. We take pride in writing solid production code in Python, deploying and supporting model services and pipelines, and pushing the boundaries in latency, throughput, and scalability.
...
As a Senior Data Scientist (Machine Learning Engineer) on the Catalog Data Quality team, you will collaborate with other Data Scientists and ML Engineers to develop, deploy, and scale machine learning models in production to extract attributes from product information. You will play a key role in supporting the attribute extraction platform and catalog data quality solutions — adding capabilities and driving adoption of GenAI-powered solutions to increase the coverage and accuracy of the Walmart's product catalog. The Data Science and ML Engineering community at Walmart Global Tech is active in most of the Hack events, utilizing the petabytes of data at our disposal, to build some of the coolest ideas. All the work we do at Walmart Global Tech will eventually benefit our operations & our associates, helping Customers Save Money to Live Better.

results.title: Dollar General appoints chief data and AI officer | Let's Data Science
highlights:
Retail Dive and Grocery Dive report that Dollar General announced nine officer appointments on June 15-16, 2026, covering information technology, artificial intelligence, store operations, real estate, and human resources. According to Retail Dive, Progressive Grocer, and Grocery Dive, Travis Nixon, who joined Dollar General in November 2025 as senior vice president of artificial intelligence optimization, was named senior vice president and chief data and artificial intelligence officer and will oversee AI strategy, delivery operations, data engineering, enablement, and enterprise business process management. Retail Dive and Progressive Grocer also report Tom Hutchins was named senior vice president and chief technology officer, and multiple promotions were made across store operations and decision-science teams. For practitioners, the elevation of a chief data and AI officer at a major value retailer underscores continued operational adoption of AI in merchandising, supply chain, and store workflows.
...
Retail Dive and Grocery Dive report that Dollar General announced nine officer appointments on June 15-16, 2026, spanning information technology, artificial intelligence, store operations, real estate, and human resources. According to Retail Dive, Progressive Grocer, and Grocery Dive, Travis Nixon, who joined the company in November 2025 as senior vice president of artificial intelligence optimization, was named senior vice president and chief data and artificial intelligence officer. Retail Dive and Progressive Grocer report the role consolidates oversight of the retailer's AI strategies and delivery operations, including data engineering, enablement, and enterprise business process management focused on merchandising, supply chain, and store operations. Progressive Grocer and Retail Dive also report Tom Hutchins moved to the role of senior vice president and chief technology officer, and the company announced promotions including Nick Snow to senior vice president of corporate store operations and Vai Anand to vice president of decision science and analytics.
...
Per Retail Dive and Grocery Dive, Nixon's responsibilities as described in the announcements include leading AI strategy, delivery functions, and continued leadership of business process management initiatives that apply AI to optimization opportunities across the retail value chain. Progressive Grocer notes Nixon's original hire in November 2025 created a dedicated AI optimization role, and Supermarket News includes an announcement quote from Steve Deckard regarding Nixon's appointment and the retailer's intent to integrate AI into operations.
...
The appointments at a large, low-cost national retailer are a practical indicator that AI efforts in retail are moving from experimentation toward operational scale, particularly in supply-chain, merchandising, and store execution workflows. Observers tracking enterprise ML adoption will view promotions in decision science and consolidated AI leadership as signals that model deployment, monitoring, and data enablement are becoming prioritized operational capabilities across retail field teams.
...
The appointment of a chief data and AI officer at a national value retailer is a notable signal that operational AI is advancing beyond pilots, affecting supply chain and store workflows relevant to practitioners. The story rates as notable rather than industry-shaking because it is an executive reorganization rather than a new technical breakthrough.

results.title: Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience at Walmart | Jeremy
highlights:
Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience at Walmart | Jeremy
...
# Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience
...
Distinguished individual contributor role leading next-generation agentic AI systems and autonomous agents at enterprise scale across Walmart's global operations, combining deep ML/AI expertise with cross-functional mentorship and high-impact productionized solutions.
...
We are seeking a Distinguished Data Scientist to provide technical leadership and long‑term vision for next‑generation AI systems and platforms. This senior individual contributor will drive breakthrough system design and advanced solution development that transforms Walmart at scale. The role combines deep technical expertise (ML, simulation, optimization, agentic AI) with cross‑functional influence, mentoring of senior talent, and delivery of high‑impact, productionized solutions. You will be a great fit if: - You architect end-to-end ML/AI systems — from feature engineering through production deployment and monitoring - You’ve built and shipped multiple autonomous AI agents (not just experimented with LLM APIs) - You’re Level 5+ on Steve Yegge’s Vibe Coding scale and can ship production systems through AI-assisted development - You combine deep statistical rigor with practical business impact
...
About the Team The Associate AI Experiences team applies advanced data science, machine learning, and agentic AI to solve high-impact business problems across Walmart globally. You’ll build production Agentic systems, deploy autonomous AI agents, and translate complex data into actionable insights that drive measurable business value at massive scale. Our team builds intelligent platforms, agents and ML-powered solutions that make associates’ jobs easier, faster, and more fulfilling across stores, fulfillment centers, distribution centers, and home office. We leverage the Element LLM Gateway to safely access state-of-the-art language models while keeping all data within Walmart’s network. Our work spans NLP, computer vision, time series forecasting, and multi-agent AI systems at enterprise scale.
...
- Build and deploy production ML systems end-to-end: data pipelines, feature stores, model training, serving layers, monitoring, and feedback loops at scale
...
- Provide technical visionand lead advanced development in agentic AI, reinforcement learning, and simulation.
...
- Architect and deploy large-scale AI systems and autonomous AI agents — conversational assistants, predictive agents with tool-calling, and multi-agent orchestration systems using LangChain, Pydantic AI, and RAG patterns
...
- Deploy NLP pipelines at enterprise scale — semantic similarity across millions of records using BERT/SBERT embeddings and vector search (FAISS)
...
- Deep expertise in ML algorithms (XGBoost, CatBoost, LightGBM, Random Forest, AutoML) and deep learning (PyTorch, transformer architectures).
...
- Production experience with NLP (BERT, SBERT, FAISS, RAG), computer vision (YOLO, CLIP), and time series forecasting (ARIMA, Prophet).
...
- Hands-on experience building and orchestrating multiple AI agents with LangChain, RAG, tool integration, and memory management.
...
- Multi-cloud ML platform proficiency — AWS SageMaker AND GCP (Vertex AI, BigQuery, BigQuery ML).
...
- Strong Python skills (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn) with containerization (Docker) and MLOps practices.
...
- Masters degree in Statistics, Economics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field AND 8+ years’ experience in data science or Machine Learning roles with demonstrated impact.
...
- PhD in AI, Machine Learning, Computer Science, Information Technology, Statistics, Applied Mathematics, Econometrics, or related field with relevant industry experience.

## Job description
...
Google's software engineers develop the next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. Our products need to handle information at massive scale, and extend well beyond web search. We're looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including information retrieval, distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking and data storage, security, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, UI design and mobile; the list goes on and is growing every day. As a software engineer, you will work on a specific project critical to Google’s needs with opportunities to switch teams and projects as you and our fast-paced business grow and evolve. We need our engineers to be versatile, display leadership qualities and be enthusiastic to take on new problems across the full-stack as we continue to push technology forward.
...

You will design, implement, and launch innovative Large Language Model (LLM) and Machine Learning (ML) systems to enhance the shopping journey for users worldwide.
